ales triq The Case for Timing-Centric Distributed Software Edward A. Lee , Slobodan Matic , Sanjit Seshia , Jia Zou Citation Edward A. Lee, Slobodan Matic, Sanjit Seshia, Jia Zou. "The Case for Timing-Centric Distributed Software". IEEE International

-Centric Distributed Software". IEEE International Conference on Distributed Computing Systems Workshops: Workshop on Cyber-Physical Systems, IEEE, 22, June, 2009. Abstract This paper makes the case that the time is right to introduce temporal seman

hat the time is right to introduce temporal semantics into programming models for cyber-physical systems. Specifically, we argue for a programming model called PTIDES that provides a coordination language rooted in discrete event semantics, suppo

language rooted in discrete event semantics, supported by a lightweight runtime framework and tools for verifying concurrent software components. PTIDES leverages recent innovations in network time synchronization to deliver distributed real-time sy

ynchronization to deliver distributed real-time systems with determinate concurrent semantics, decentralized and robust control, and the potential for rigorous schedulability analysis. Electronic downloads LeeMaticSeshiaZou_Ptides.pdf · application/p
